thm:1.4.1.summary: For $G$ a $p$-divisible group over $R$ quasi-syntomic, the pair $(\mathcal{M}_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}(G) = \mathcal{E}xt^1(G, \mathcal{O}^{\mathrm{pris}}), \varphi_{\mathcal{M}})$ is an admissible prismatic Dieudonné module. Defines the prismatic Dieudonné functor.

thm:1.4.4.summary: Main theorem (intro): the prismatic Dieudonné functor $\mathcal{M}_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}: G \mapsto (\mathcal{M}_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}(G), \varphi)$ is an antiequivalence on quasi-syntomic rings. Headline classification result.

thm:3.1.6.summary: Bhatt–Scholze Hodge-Tate comparison (imported, BS thm:4.10): $\eta_R^*: \Omega^*_{R/(A/I)} \to H^*(\overline{\mathbbl{\Delta}}_{R/A})$ extends. Foundation for the prismatic Dieudonné apparatus.

thm:3.1.7.summary: Bhatt–Scholze crystalline comparison (imported, BS thm:5.2): $\mathbbl{\Delta}_{R^{(1)}/A} \simeq R\Gamma_{\mathrm{crys}}(R/A)$. The char-$p$ specialization the Dieudonné theory runs on.

thm:3.3.9.summary: André's lemma (imported, BS thm:7.14): for $R$ perfectoid, exists $p$-completely faithfully flat $R \to S$ of perfectoids with $S$ absolutely integrally closed. The flatness input.

thm:3.4.4.summary: For QRS-perfectoid $R$: $\mathcal{N}^{\ge i}/\mathcal{N}^{\ge i+1}(\mathbbl{\Delta}_R) \cong \mathrm{Fil}^{\mathrm{conj}}_i(\overline{\mathbbl{\Delta}}_R)\{i\}$. Nygaard graded matches conjugate filtration on Hodge-Tate side; structural backbone of the Dieudonné identification.

thm:4.3.2.summary: For $R$ quasi-syntomic with $pR = 0$ and $G$ a $p$-divisible group: $\mathcal{M}_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}(G) \cong v_*^{\mathrm{crys}}(\mathcal{E}xt^1_{(R/\mathbb{Z}_p)_{\mathrm{crys}}}(...))$ Frobenius-equivariantly. Connects prismatic Dieudonné to classical crystalline Dieudonné.

thm:4.4.1.summary: Resolution: every abelian group $G$ in the relevant category admits a functorial resolution by polynomial $\mathbb{Z}[X_i]$ where each $X_i$ is a finite disjoint union. Homological-algebra input enabling Ext computations.

thm:4.5.4.summary: Prismatic-cohomology vanishing for abelian schemes: $\mathcal{E}xt^i_{(R)_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}}(u^{-1}(X), \overline{\mathcal{O}}_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}) = 0$ for $i \ne ?$ for abelian $X/R$. Computational input.

thm:4.5.6.summary: Companion vanishing on the integral $\mathcal{O}_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}$ side: $\mathcal{E}xt^i_{(R)_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}}(u^{-1}(X), \mathcal{O}_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}) = 0$ for $i$ outside the relevant range.

thm:4.6.1.summary: Embedding theorem (imported): every finite locally free group scheme over $S$ embeds Zariski-locally in a (projective) abelian scheme. Lets Dieudonné theory of finite group schemes be reduced to abelian schemes.

thm:4.6.7.summary: For $R$ quasi-syntomic and $G$ a $p$-divisible group: $(\mathcal{M}_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}(G), \varphi)$ is admissible — i.e. lies in the right module category. Proves admissibility for the body version of the main theorem.

thm:4.6.10.summary: Main antiequivalence (body): for $R$ quasi-syntomic, $\mathcal{M}_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}: \mathrm{BT}(R) \to \mathrm{DM}^{\mathrm{adm}}(R)$ is an antiequivalence between $p$-divisible groups and admissible prismatic Dieudonné modules. Body proof of thm:1.4.4.

thm:4.8.1.summary: For $R$ quasi-regular semiperfectoid: prismatic Dieudonné functor is fully faithful for $p$-divisible groups. The fully-faithful half on QRSP rings, used in proving thm:4.6.10.

thm:4.9.5.summary: For $R$ quasi-regular semiperfectoid: full antiequivalence on QRSP rings. Combined with descent extends to thm:4.6.10.

thm:5.1.4.summary: Natural exact antiequivalence $H \mapsto (M_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}(H), \varphi, \psi)$ (with inverse also exact). The fully-faithful + essentially-surjective version of the main theorem at the level of finite group schemes.

thm:5.2.5.summary: For complete regular local $R$ with perfect residue field of char $p$: functor $\mathrm{BT}(R) \to \mathrm{BK}_{\mathrm{min}}(A)$, $G \mapsto v^* \mathcal{M}_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}(G)((A,I))$. Identifies prismatic Dieudonné with Breuil–Kisin minimal modules over a regular local ring.

edges.note: Intro statement is the body theorem about admissibility.
Main intro theorem is the body antiequivalence.
Nygaard graded match runs on Bhatt–Scholze Hodge-Tate comparison.
Crystalline comparison machinery underlies the conjugate-filtration computation.
Prismatic-vs-crystalline Dieudonné comparison uses the Nygaard graded structure.
Vanishing computations use the functorial resolution.
Integral version of the structure-sheaf vanishing.
Antiequivalence requires admissibility on the target side.
Antiequivalence reduces to QRSP via descent.
Embedding theorem reduces finite group schemes to abelian schemes.
Fully-faithful on QRSP via crystalline-Dieudonné identification.
André's flatness lemma supplies the descent input.
Full antiequivalence extends fully-faithfulness on QRSP.
Finite-group-scheme antiequivalence specializes the $p$-divisible group case.
BK-minimal-module identification extends antiequivalence to a regular local ring setting.

main_results.description: Section 1.4, 4.6, 5.1. Definition + main antiequivalence theorem on quasi-syntomic rings; finite-group-scheme version.

imported_BS_machinery.description: Section 3. Hodge-Tate comparison, crystalline comparison, André's flatness — the Bhatt–Scholze prismatic foundation imported.

Nygaard_and_Dieudonne.description: Sections 3.4, 4.3. Nygaard graded matches conjugate filtration; prismatic Dieudonné = crystalline Dieudonné in char $p$.

homological_apparatus.description: Section 4.4–4.6. Functorial resolutions, Ext-vanishing for abelian schemes, embedding finite group schemes, admissibility.

QRSP_antiequivalence.description: Sections 4.8, 4.9. Fully-faithful + full antiequivalence on QRSP rings — the technical heart from which the general result is descended.

applications.description: Section 5.2. Identification with Breuil–Kisin minimal modules over regular local rings.

Prismatic.title: Prisms and prismatic cohomology
Prismatic.role: Foundation: Hodge-Tate comparison, crystalline comparison, André's flatness all imported.

ScholzeWeinstein.title: Moduli of p-divisible groups
ScholzeWeinstein.role: Predecessor on $p$-divisible groups in the perfectoid framework. Companion result (geometry side).

BMS1.title: Integral p-adic Hodge theory
BMS1.role: Breuil–Kisin–Fargues module classification (BMS1 thm:4.28) is parallel-mechanism to thm:5.2.5.

Faltings:Almost.title: Almost étale extensions
Faltings:Almost.role: Almost-mathematics foundation in the prismatic Dieudonné apparatus.
